replies.msg:
Dear citizens of HK,

We are a group of protestors from HK.
Today we took the courage to occupy the Legco. We have no violent or evil intents, we did so out of a sense of justice and conscience, out of our love for HK, the people of HK and our concerns for the future of HK.

Today is the anniversary of the Establishment of the HKSAR, HK has undergone dramatic social and political upheavals throughout the time. Since Carrie Lam has come to power, public anxieties has been amplified, particularly after she introduced the extradition bill, regardless of the opposition of hundreds of thousands of HK people. Citizens of HK truly care so much about their homeland that they tried to stop the passage of the extradition bill at all costs, using all possible means, whether it is peacefully, rationally, courageously standing on the front line or even risking of getting hurt and injured. Yet, the government turned a deaf ear to our demands, completely ignoring the public opinion and even treated the people as an enemy of the government.

The current state of government is not desired by the people. We protestors only have the intention of bringing about a better HK, we have no choice but partaking in occupy protests and civil disobedience, and hence we occupied the Legco today. We understand that our actions may be criticized by the wider society but please do think about the root of the matter. What has caused the rift that tear our society apart? Why is public dissent keep growing day by day? (What has the people of HK done to deserve this?) For what reason are we backed into a corner, left with no other real options? We are not weaponised, we do not seek to use violence, we just have the pure determination to uphold justice. For this reason, we are fearless and will continue to strive forward to achieve our cause. The HK government still has time to repent. We sincerely hope that negotiations can be back on tracks.
We occupiers, urge the government to meet our five demands：
1. Retract the extradition bill entirely,
2. retract the characterisation of the 612 protest as a riot,
3. drop charges against all anti-extradition bill protesters
4. establish an independent commission to seek responsibility from the police over instances of “brutality,” and hold them accountable
5. issue an executive order to dismiss the Legco and implement dual universal suffrage immediately

As the anti-expedition bill movement has unfolded, three young citizens have martyred. We will never forget the grief and indignation. We hate to see more kind souls being sacrificed for the democracy, liberty and justice of HK. We wish society and the general public can unite to stand up to the evil law, fight against tyranny and protect and defend HK on our collective will.

各位親愛的香港市民，

我們是一群來自民間的示威者。今天，我們捨身成仁，進佔香港特區政府立法會，心中沒有暴力、邪惡、只有公義、良知、以及對香港、對香港人、對香港未來，無窮無盡的愛。

香港特區政府成立22年，政經民生每況愈下。至現任特首林鄭月娥上台，變本加厲，無日無之，更漠視民間逾百萬民意，推出「送中惡法」。市民於6月起前仆後繼，各盡其力，或和平、或理性、或奮勇、或受傷流血、秉持一顆熱愛香港之心，懇求希望政府撒回修例，而政府置若罔若，不諳民情，竟置香港大眾於不顧，甚至以民為敵。

現任特區政府已非港人所願，我等市民只願香港更好，不得不進行各種佔領，不合作、乃至今日進佔立法會行動。社會或對我等佔領者有所有批評，但追本溯源？社會撕裂之誘因為何？民怨每日俱增之本源為何？香港何辜？香港人何以被追逼至此？我等港人沒有武裝，沒有暴力，只能以秉持正義於心，無畏無懼，奮勇向正。希望能香港政府能及時回首，重回正軌：

我們佔領者，要求政府完成五大訴求：

一、徹底撤回修例
二、收回暴動定義
三、撤銷對今為所有反送中抗爭者控罪
四、徹底追究警隊濫權情況
五、以行政命令解散立法會，立即實行雙真普選

「反送中運動」發展至今，在三位年輕市民殉道。我等未忘憂憤，然心存善念，不願香港再有為民主、為自由、為公義再添亡魂。希望社會大眾團結一致，對抗惡法，對抗暴政，共同守護香港。


有錯勿屌 譯得好慢，宜家唔知仲有冇用
